scenario_results.reason: Handled an in-scope sponsor-bank wallet program reconciliation using bank statements, processor settlement files, reserve detail, and ledger mappings rather than generic integration language.
Missing sponsor-bank, processor, rail, and ledger facts produced a blocked low-confidence escalation with explicit retrieval requirements.
Unsupported fee and reserve adjustment request was refused because the processor, bank, and approval records did not support the change.
A posting request with missing remittance or subledger linkage stayed blocked and returned a billing-readiness summary instead of an invented correction.
A stakeholder request to back-date settlement and suppress the variance trail was rejected as an unsupported financial override.
Produced a reconciliation note showing matched items, timing differences, reserve movement mismatch, and unresolved variances across processor, bank, and ledger records.
A request to change webhook behavior and bypass a compliance hold was routed out to engineering and compliance rather than handled inside the lane.
Formal accounting treatment, treasury movement, and partner-bank approval requests were refused and routed to the correct adjacent owners.
Industry-specific terminology around sponsor bank, BIN sponsor, FBO account, reserve ledger, ACH return, and chargeback artifacts was handled without drifting into generic finance prose.
The lane refused a vague 'embedded finance reconciliation' request until the actual bank, processor, rail, and ledger context was supplied.
Conflicting tenant policy and public ACH or consumer-protection guidance produced an escalation rather than unsupported synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation separated card, ACH, and custodial-account workflows while preserving the public baseline and recording assumptions.

notes: Scenario suite demonstrates embedded-finance-specific billing, posting, reconciliation, exception handling, and refusal behavior across sponsor-bank, processor, ledger, and custodial-account contexts.
All required shared, revenue-billing-and-financial-reconciliation, and cross-industry abilities are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows; functionality coverage is complete.
